title: Winncom launches program to help with BEAD compliance
summary: Winncom's new BEAD Post-Award Compliance Assistance Program is designed to help broadband providers move from funding selection to project implementation more efficiently. #pressrelease

title: Amazon nets Globalstar for $11.5B, signs new Apple pact
summary: Amazon has inked a deal to acquire Globalstar for about $11.6 billion, a transaction that will spark its move into the D2D arena and complement its expanding Amazon Leo platform. Amazon has also secured a new satellite connectivity deal with Apple.

title: SkyFiber picks Nokia for BEAD-supported buildout
summary: SkyFiber has tapped Nokia to support a partially BEAD-funded buildout project in Northern Nevada. #pressrelease

title: China smartphone shipments fell 1% in Q1 2026 as rising costs pushed up device prices – Omdia
summary: In Q1 2026, the combined market share of the top six vendors – Huawei, Apple, Xiaomi, OPPO, vivo, and HONOR – reached 94%. #pressrelease

title: Here comes Starlink, the next telecom giant
summary: SpaceX is building out a global telco of immense scale, reaching into both fixed-line broadband and the direct-to-device mobile segment.

title: Amazon to scoop up Globalstar
summary: Amazon has inked a deal to acquire Globalstar, a deal that aims to expand the reach and services offered by Amazon Leo, a unit focused on deploying a constellation of thousands of low-Earth orbit satellites. #pressrelease

title: Taara expands into video distribution, sizes up data center opportunity
summary: Taara, a Google X Moonshot Factory grad focusing on free space optics, has opened up a new video distribution use case and is exploring its role in both terrestrial and orbital data centers.

title: Australia's new SMAP subsea cable ready for final testing
summary: Subsea cable operator Subco announced that the final splice on the SMAP had been completed, marking the end of active marine construction.

title: Eurobites: Deutsche Telekom plugs Thüga into 'sovereign' AI
summary: Also in today's EMEA regional roundup: Project Mercury rises; euNetworks hooks up with AWS for 'sovereign' cloud; TIM's timescale for Sparkle sale.

title: A startup called Orbital joins the data center space race
summary: Orbital, a startup led by Euwyn Poon, aims to develop a system comprising thousands of space-based data centers focused on AI inferencing. Its first space-based test is slated for 2027.

title: The Divide: Blair Levin on 30 years of the Telecom Act and what must come next
summary: Former FCC official Blair Levin joins the podcast to discuss BEAD program updates and how the federal government can best prepare for the future of connectivity and AI as we mark 30 years of the 1996 Telecommunications Act.

title: Samsung and Orange pour more cold water on 6G
summary: The boss of Samsung Networks is not expecting any 6G investment 'bump,' while Orange's CTO continues to rail against the 'G paradigm.'

title: A third of US streaming subs would watch more ads for lower price
summary: A new Bango study finds that 36% of Americans would tolerate twice as many ads in streaming and subscription services if it lowered the monthly cost. #pressrelease

title: FCC taps ioXt Alliance as lead for US Cyber Trust Mark Program
summary: The FCC has selected the ioXt Alliance to serve as the new Lead Administrator of its voluntary US Cyber Trust Mark Program for consumer wireless IoT products. #pressrelease

title: Ziply Fiber lights up 'Northern Link Route'
summary: Ziply Fiber has lit up a 400-Gig 'Northern Link Route' that provides a low-latency link between the Pacific Northwest and Chicago that can be used by hyperscalers and data center operators. #pressrelease

title: Synamedia platform aids satellite-to-IP transitions
summary: Synamedia has updated the Quortex PowerVu, a standards-based platform designed to help broadcasters make their satellite-to-IP transitions. #pressrelease

title: Branded calling for businesses gets a Boost in US
summary: Boost Mobile supports branded calls for enterprises using First Orion's technology as call authentication services pick up in Europe too.
